text: On the coupled Burgers-swept-KdV system, a smoothed bore in u and a rightward KdV-like soliton in v at x=-8: as the soliton encounters the bore, does it transmit (refract), reflect, fuse, or get destroyed? Final v must retain a peak >= 0.5 with |u_max| < 5 at T=8.

method: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule dealiasing on all quadratic products + explicit classical RK4; NO u-side viscosity, NO hyperviscosity, NO operator splitting (baseline)
bank_use_rationale: Negative bank dictates baseline shape: rejecting kb-burgers-fwdEuler-centralFD-Gibbs / kb-shallowWater-centralFD-fwdEuler-hNegative / kb-general-centralFD-hyperbolic-shockFormation rules out central-FD on advective u u_x — but spectral derivatives are still pure differentiation (no upwinding), so this baseline keeps Gibbs risk as the expected E1 failure mode. Rejecting kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts and BKdV-S1 forces 2/3-rule dealiasing (NOT considered a 'complex' upgrade — it is the minimum needed to keep quadratics from NaN in O(t<0.5)). Rejecting kb-kdv-explicit-RK4-stiffness-blowup mandates dt within RK4 v_xxx CFL (~4.9e-4 post-dealias k_max); dt=2e-4 matches BKdV-S1 R2 safe regime. We deliberately DO NOT add u-viscosity in E1 even though BKdV-S6 warns of bore corruption — that is the next-iteration escalation. We DO NOT use LxF (kb-burgers-LaxFriedrichs-longTime-dissipation: over-diffuses bore) or MUSCL/Godunov here (multi-component upgrade beyond E1 minimal scope).

rationale: Exactly the BKdV-S6 failure mode: bore self-steepens explosively (u_max 1.5 -> 11.4 transient, 9.66 final; TV(u) 1.5 -> 320, ~213x), v develops 11 spurious peaks, u_min crosses -4. Phenomenon target fails on |u_max|<5 (got 9.66) and on 'bore not blown up' (it has, in the BKdV-S6 sense of TV explosion and high-k Gibbs cascade). v amplitude > 0.5 was met by accident via fragmentation. No NaN — finiteness alone is misleading (kb-general-finiteness-not-accuracy).

Failure mode is exactly what BKdV-S6 deep-synthesis predicted: bare Fourier+2/3-dealias+RK4 cannot bound the Burgers self-flux on a bore IC. The single-component upgrade per BKdV-S6's recommended_alternative is to add explicit linear viscosity nu*u_xx on the u-equation; the synthesis quotes nu ~ 5e-2 as the empirical clean threshold (with nu*k_max^2 >> u_max*k_max ~ 0.17). Explicitly REJECT the BKdV-S4 safe-envelope hyperviscosity nu_h~1e-22: BKdV-S6 establishes that bound is 13 orders of magnitude too weak for bore-like u-IC.

method: Same as E1 (Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule dealiasing + explicit RK4) PLUS single-component upgrade: explicit linear viscosity nu*u_xx on the u-equation only, nu=5e-2
bank_use_rationale: Single-component upgrade vs E1: add nu*u_xx on u only (treated explicitly inside RK4). BKdV-S6 deep synthesis: nu=5e-2 empirically passes (TV_final ~ 9.6, u_min_final >= 0) on the EXACT same bore IC used here, while the alternative hyperviscosity nu_h must approach the explicit-RK4 stability ceiling ~1e-9 (10+ orders above BKdV-S4 'safe envelope' 1e-22). REJECT BKdV-S4 ladder: nu_h<=1e-12 is empirically insufficient (TV>115, u_max>3) — would waste an iteration. REJECT MUSCL/Godunov on u u_x: a 2+ component swap from E1 (changes both discretization AND adds limiter) violates progressive-complexity discipline at this stage. REJECT LxF (kb-burgers-LaxFriedrichs-longTime-dissipation, kb-shallowWater-LaxFriedrichs-overdiffusion): smears the bore and would mis-time the encounter. CFL check: nu*k_max^2*dt ~ 5e-2 * (pi*256/30)^2 * 2e-4 ~ 7.2e-3 << 2.78, comfortable inside explicit-RK4 diffusion stability.

rationale: All phenomenon targets met. (1) |u_max| stays < 5 throughout (peak 3.33 around t<=2, final 2.18). (2) v retains v_max >= 0.56 across the entire run, v_max_final = 0.63 > 0.5. (3) Bore not blown up: TV(u) bounded between 20 and 40 across the run (vs 320 in E1) — the bore steepens then dissipates into a viscous shock layer of consistent width. (4) Mass conserved to FFT round-off. The 6 small peaks in v indicate the soliton (initially at x=-8) has propagated rightward, encountered the bore, partially transmitted with some radiation/fission — a physically interpretable interaction (not destruction). Single-component upgrade from E1 worked exactly as BKdV-S6 deep-synthesis predicted; no need for E3.

E2 meets all phenomenon targets with a clean, defensible single-component upgrade over the E1 baseline. Spending E3 on, e.g., MUSCL/Godunov on u u_x or higher-order time integration would not change the qualitative answer and could not be motivated by an unresolved failure mode in F2. Stop early.
